{"step_by_step_thinking": "ADP-ribosylation is a post-translational modification (PTM) that involves the addition of an ADP-ribose group to proteins. This modification is catalyzed by enzymes such as poly(ADP-ribose) polymerases (PARPs). In the UMLS document (Document [29]), it is explicitly mentioned that 'hypoxanthine is specifically characterized as a PARP inhibitory agent that blocks poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase activity.' This indicates that ADP-ribosylation is a biological process involving PARP enzymes, which are directly linked to the modification of proteins. Furthermore, in the context of epigenetic mechanisms, Document [12] states that 'additional post-translational modifications to histone proteins that affect gene expression include ubiquitination, phosphorylation, and ADP-ribosylation.' This directly classifies ADP-ribosylation as a post-translational modification. Therefore, based on the provided documents, ADP-ribosylation is clearly recognized as a PTM.", "answer_choice": "A"}
